entities.name: Vrijen Attawar
entities.context: Founder of Careerspan, career coach and tech entrepreneur

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: Building a candidate-centric layer on top of existing hiring infrastructure

entities.name: Candidate-Centric Hiring
entities.context: Capturing deep candidate data once and reusing it across many jobs to reduce repetitive application experiences

entities.name: Quality Over Volume
entities.context: Careerspan's value proposition: quality and leverage per candidate minute invested, not brute-force volume

entities.name: Auto-Apply
entities.context: Differentiated capability where Careerspan prepares applications for candidates using existing profile knowledge

entities.name: Workday
entities.context: Referenced as an example of repetitive, demoralizing application systems that Careerspan aims to improve upon

entities.name: Candidate Profile Reuse
entities.context: Same underlying candidate profile matched to multiple roles, leveraging stories, skills, and preferences

relationships.context: Founder building Careerspan's candidate-centric hiring platform
Core architectural approach: capture data once, reuse across jobs
Value proposition explicitly inverts industry norm of optimizing for applicant quantity
Auto-apply flow leverages existing candidate knowledge (stories, skills, preferences) to prepare applications
Careerspan positions itself against repetitive systems like Workday
Proposed differentiated capability: 'we prepared this application for you' flow
Quality-per-minute philosophy is the economic logic behind candidate-centric design
